$comment: Ch 3 — THE FADE, anchor scenario (Dirk archetype, FLAMINGO body shape). Authored from SCENARIO_SPECS_v1.md + SCENARIO_VOICE_v1.md (locked 2026-05-21). Per BASKETBALL_JOURNEY v2 §7, voice uses body shape ('FLAMINGO. OVER THE REACH.'), never the athlete's name.

meta.title: FLAMINGO. OVER THE REACH.
meta.subtitle: CH 3 — THE FADE, ANCHOR
meta.authorIntent: The one-legged fadeaway over a contesting hand. d=6.0 (full meter of fade beyond a normal jumper), h_r=2.5 (tall release), h_h=3.05. Textbook answer θ=58°, v=8.33 m/s. Verification: t = 6.0 / (8.33·cos 58°) = 1.359 s; y(1.359) = 2.5 + 8.33·sin(58°)·1.359 − ½·9.8·1.359² = 3.050 m — matches h_h (err 0.000 m). Cultural framing is body shape ('FLAMINGO') + the moment ('OVER THE REACH'), not the athlete — per BASKETBALL_JOURNEY v2 §7. Hardest version of the fade lesson: the body is doing several wrong things at once, and the answer still has to land.

situation.questionRevealed: Find an angle θ and velocity v that lands the ball through the hoop.

subheadVariants.1: Came up short. One foot is one foot of power lost.
subheadVariants.3+: Short again. Power well below threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Front iron. The fading body stole the arc.
subheadVariants.2: Still front iron. Lift the arc.
subheadVariants.3+: Front iron again. Push the angle higher.

subheadVariants.1: Back iron. Overcompensated for the drift.
subheadVariants.2: Still back iron. Cut the velocity.

subheadVariants.1: Long. Too much arm against a small body.
subheadVariants.3+: Far overshoot. Power well above threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Air ball. The body went backward, the ball followed.
subheadVariants.2: Air ball again. Aim for the rim.
subheadVariants.3+: Still air ball. Check angle and velocity.

ghostArc.description: Textbook answer θ=58°, v=8.33 m/s. The high release plus the strong arc make this the unblockable shot of the chapter — visibly the shape that earned the FLAMINGO name.

hints.body: The fade adds distance and the high release adds height. Both go in the equations.

Two equations, two unknowns:
  x(t) = v·cos(θ)·t
  y(t) = h_r + v·sin(θ)·t − ½·g·t²
d ≈ 6 m, h_r ≈ 2.5 m. Use them.
Substitute t = d/(v·cos θ) into y. Try θ near 58°. The high release helps — but the distance asks for v above 8.

workedSteps: Solve y(t) = h_h for v → v ≈ 8.33 m/s.

intro.headline: FLAMINGO. OVER THE REACH.
intro.subhead: A fading 7-footer over an outstretched hand. The body is moving wrong.

subheadByFlavor.GLASS: Square caught a fading ball. Held it in.
subheadByFlavor.RIM_DROP: Iron grabbed it on the way past. Stayed.

miss.headline: OFF THE FADE

diagnosticByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Front iron. The fading body stole the arc.
diagnosticByCategory.BACK_RIM: Back iron. Overcompensated for the drift.
diagnosticByCategory.SHORT: Came up short. One foot is one foot of power lost.
diagnosticByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Long. Too much arm against a small body.
diagnosticByCategory.AIRBALL: Air ball. The body went backward, the ball followed.

bracketHintByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Geometry: θ near 58°, push v near 8.3.
bracketHintByCategory.BACK_RIM: Geometry: θ near 58°, cut v by ~0.4.
bracketHintByCategory.SHORT: Geometry: θ near 58°, push v near 8.3.
bracketHintByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Geometry: θ near 58°, cut v by ~0.8.

miss.afterAllHintsCopy: All hints revealed. The math is on the table.
